core_model.structure_summary: A continuous-time first-order flow that moves a state down the gradient of a scalar function preconditioned by the inverse of a symmetric positive-definite metric operator (the Hessian of a potential), whose forward-Euler-type discretizations recover a proximal/Bregman update.
core_model.canonical_form:
\dot{x}(t) = -H(x(t))^{-1}
abla F(x(t)),\quad H=
abla^2\psi;\qquad 	ext{discrete: } 
abla\psi(x_{k+1})=
abla\psi(x_k)-\eta
abla F(x_k)\;\Leftrightarrow\; x_{k+1}=\arg\min_{x}\;\eta\langle x,
abla F(x_k)angle+D_\psi(x,x_k)

variables.meaning: point being evolved/optimized in the domain
continuous flow time, discretized into step index
scalar function whose gradient drives the flow
metric operator = Hessian of a strictly convex potential (defines the geometry and Bregman divergence)

notes: Genuinely dual object: the novel unifying structure is the Riemannian/Hessian-metric gradient-flow ODE, but the paper is equally about its discretizations (the mirror-descent proximal/Bregman update and natural-gradient-descent update), which could be classified object_type 'optimization' or 'discrete_update'. Chose 'ode' because the central results characterize the discrete methods as partial vs full discretizations of this flow. When H is not a Hessian, the Bregman/dual structure is dropped and only the metric-flow remains.
